steps.title: Identify the gap pattern between treatments
steps.content: The key signal is the gap between the ethylene-treated bar (light) and untreated bar (dark) at each temperature. Notice that this gap is much larger at 14°C, 16°C, and 18°C than at 20°C.

steps.title: Match the gap pattern to the answer
steps.content:
At the three lower temperatures, the gap is roughly 3–3.5 days each; at 20°C, it shrinks to about 1.5 days.

This reveals that ethylene's absolute effect on ripening time was much stronger at lower temperatures.

D states exactly this pattern: ethylene caused a greater absolute change at 14°C, 16°C, and 18°C than at 20°C.

A, B, and C either misstate the data or reverse the observed relationships.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
